text.title: Competitive UX Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Competitive UX Analysis. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Rubric criteria and weights are agreed before evaluation begins; ad-hoc rubric changes during evaluation invalidate the comparison.
rule.rationale: Locked rubric prevents post-hoc cherry-picking of criteria that favour a result.

rule.statement: Evaluation uses a real or sandbox account with realistic data, not landing-page screenshots.
Three to seven competitors are evaluated; below three the picture is anecdotal, above seven the rubric becomes unmaintainable.
rule.rationale: Outside this range the report either lacks coverage or drowns in detail.

rule.statement: Every rubric score cites at least one screenshot or recording timestamp; bare scores are rejected.
rule.rationale: Bare scores cannot survive cross-team scrutiny.

rule.statement: Output ends with a positioning recommendation (gap to exploit / parity to maintain / table-stakes to copy), not raw scores.
rule.rationale: Score tables without recommendations rarely drive decisions.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-competitive-analysis.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
